name: Onion Soup Fondue
recipeInstructions:
Note: Bread should be sliced 1-inch thick.
Melt butter in a large kettle over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until transparent but not browned. Stir in beef broth and chicken stock base. Simmer, uncovered, for 2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and refrigerate overnight or for several hours. Before serving, discard any chilled surface fat that has formed. Reheat the soup gently and season with white pepper to taste. Slice the cheese into 12 pieces. Lightly toast 12 bread slices and place them in individual ovenproof serving bowls. Top each bowl with one slice of Jack cheese. Spoon the heated soup into the bowls, then add a piece of bread and another slice of cheese on top. Place the bowls under a broiler for just until the cheese is bubbly and soft but not browned.
